feat: streamline left drawer — dynamic buttons only, accent color, named sessions

Left drawer now contains only dynamic command buttons and the +/− controls.
The utility section (Export Screen, Make Script, Pkg Update) has been removed.

Changes:
- activity_termux.xml: removed drawer_util_row and drawer_util_divider; set
  left drawer height to wrap_content + gravity start|top so the panel grows
  and shrinks as buttons are added or removed
- TermuxActivity: removed setupDrawerUtilityButtons() call and method
- applyFeatureSettings(): removed all drawer utility visibility toggles
- setupDrawerCommandButtons(): each MaterialButton now gets
  setBackgroundTintList(accentColor); +/− OutlinedButtons get accent stroke
  and text color; applyAccentColor() calls setupDrawerCommandButtons() so
  colors update live when the accent is changed
- Button click now calls addNewSession(false, label) to open a new terminal
  session named after the button's visible label, then posts the command write
  so the command runs in that new session

commit.message:
fix: replace static shutdown-handler with closure, verify nonce coverage (10f + §5)

10f – Shutdown-handler:
- Remove static $shutdown_lock_key property and release_lock_on_shutdown()
  static method from EFS_Batch_Processor.
- Register an inline static closure (capturing $lock_uuid + $migration_id
  by value via 'use') instead. No class-reference dependency means the
  closure executes safely even when a fatal error unloads the class.
- The finally block in process_batch() still releases the lock on the
  normal path; the closure is a no-op there because lock_uuid is already
  NULL after the finally UPDATE (WHERE lock_uuid = %s matches 0 rows).

§5 – Nonce verification (documentation only, no code changes):
- Full AJAX audit confirmed every handler calls verify_request()
  (check_ajax_referer) or directly calls check_ajax_referer().
- REST endpoints correctly use WP-native permission callbacks.
- Updated optimierungen.md to reflect actual complete coverage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add v2 anti-overfitting retraining pipeline with phonetic misspeller and LoRA support

New modules:
- phonetic_misspeller.py: CMU dict phoneme perturbation, keyboard typos,
  visual similarity, creative phonetic spelling, 319 real-word subs
- generate_diverse_texts.py: 25 genres (DnD, Minecraft, school, diary, etc.)
- build_training_data.py: balanced data pipeline (60/20/10/10 composition)
- train_qwen3_lora_nibi.slurm: LoRA/QLoRA training for 0.6B-32B models

Updated:
- inject_errors.py: integrated 4 phonetic rules (31 total, up from 27)
- finetune_qwen3.py: LoRA/QLoRA, early stopping, weight_decay=0.05, 2 epochs
- evaluate_models.py: real-world eval with Pascal DnD + Bob story holdouts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add METEOR TYPING game to public directory

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: sistema de logging centralizado en Supabase (app_logs)

Logger que escribe directo a Supabase (no al backend) para capturar
errores incluso cuando Render free tier está en cold start.

- logger.js: nuevo módulo con session_id por tab (sessionStorage),
  setLoggerUser() para vincular logs a usuarios autenticados,
  logger.timed() para capturar duración de cualquier operación async,
  y captura global de unhandledRejection.

- AuthContext.jsx: logs en cada etapa del flujo de auth
  (sesion_iniciada, profile_cargado, retry_programado, fallo_definitivo)

- api.js: timing automático en todos los requests HTTP
  (método, path, status, ms de duración)

Tabla requerida en Supabase (ejecutar en SQL Editor):
  CREATE TABLE app_logs (id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    session_id text NOT NULL, user_id uuid REFERENCES auth.users(id),
    level text CHECK (level IN ('debug','info','warn','error')),
    context text NOT NULL, message text NOT NULL, data jsonb,
    url text, created_at timestamptz DEFAULT now());
  + indexes + RLS (ver PROGRESS.md o el commit message completo)

Backend Security:

- Focus trapping in ConfirmDialog and NewsletterModal

commit.message:
Fix fds_to_keep crash: pre-init tqdm multiprocessing lock

tqdm creates a multiprocessing.RLock in __new__ which spawns the
resource tracker subprocess via fork_exec.  Inside Textual's worker
threads the fd table is redirected, so Python 3.14 rejects the fds.

Pre-initializing the lock in main() before app.run() ensures fds are
still in a valid state when the resource tracker forks.

commit.message:
Reduce recording bitrate from 2.5 Mbps to 0.8 Mbps

Screen recordings of a static UI don't need 2.5 Mbps. At 2.5 Mbps a
~5-minute session produced >50MB, which hit the Supabase storage bucket
file size limit and caused ERR_SSL_BAD_RECORD_MAC_ALERT on upload.

At 0.8 Mbps a 5-minute session is ~30MB — clear of the 50MB limit and
still legible quality for UX research recordings.

commit.message:
Fix CI: create .env before composer install, use --no-dev

The 500 errors were caused by Redis not being available on CI.
Clockwork middleware (dev dependency) triggered cache access using
RedisStore before the route handler's try/catch could run.

Fix: create .env first (so artisan commands during post-autoload-dump
have correct config), install without dev dependencies (removes
Clockwork), and clear config cache after .env creation.

fix: avoid global Configuration singleton race in read_config_map (#764)

Each call to read_config_map was mutating the kubernetes_asyncio global
Configuration._default via load_incluster_config(). Concurrent async
requests could race each other, leaving the global transiently empty so
that CoreV1Api() would construct an ApiClient with no host/token → 401.

Fix by passing an explicit client_configuration object to
load_incluster_config/load_kube_config and binding ApiClient to it
directly, so no shared global state is touched.

commit.message:
fix: copy @prisma/client to schema-level node_modules for prisma resolution

Prisma resolves @prisma/client from the schema file's directory, not
from CWD or the binary location. In pnpm workspace, the schema package
may not have @prisma/client in its local node_modules. We copy it there
before running prisma generate.

Previous approaches that failed:
- Symlink in .pnpm store (prisma uses custom fs-based resolution)
- NODE_OPTIONS --require preload (prisma doesn't use Module._resolveFilename)
- NODE_PATH (same reason)
